name: Charles Lloyd
abstract: Charles Lloyd (born March 15, 1938) is an American jazz musician and composer. He primarily plays tenor saxophone and flute and occasionally other reed instruments, including alto saxophone and the Hungarian tárogató.

interviews.title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: A Tribute to "Trane"
Zakir Hussain, Tabla, and Friends
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Zakir Hussain Eternal: Celebrating 75 Years of Genius
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I talked to Charles about forming a new band... I was still with Charles Lloyd. We were off at that period... we started doing Charles with the quartet... The Charles Lloyd Quartet became a great band that I wanted to stay with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: By now I’m playing literally around the world with Keith Jarrett, Jack DeJohnette, and Charles Lloyd.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I heard you probably in around 1968 or so, with Charles Lloyd.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Eventually I took lessons with Charles Lloyd... I spent the year with Charles. I became his gofer, his aide-de-camp... there were things that I gleaned from being around him, because he was a very intelligent teacher-type guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael White
about_quotes.evidence: So I’d go to a concert with them, you know, playing King Oliver, the next night they’re playing Charles Lloyd or something and they sound like that, you know, so that was amazing to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael White interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 10, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Joanne Brackeen
about_quotes.evidence: Joanne Brackeen was born in Ventura, California on July 26, 1938. A self- taught jazz pianist, she worked in Los Angeles with Dexter Gordon and Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.doc_title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001
